perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Confidential Information as it uses for its own confidential, proprietary and trade secret information, but in no case us e less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall use less degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Confidential Information as it uses for its own confidential, proprietary and trade secret information, and in no case use more than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original intent of Section 2. The original text mandates the Recipient to use at least a reasonable degree of care, and ideally the same level of care as they use for their own confidential information. The modified text implies that the Recipient can use less care than they use for their own confidential information, contradicting the intention of protecting confidential information. Also, in case the recipient uses more care than the 'reasonable' amount, then it would be a breach. This creates uncertainty about the level of care the Recipient is expected to provide, thus the in-text contradiction is on Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PERIOD OF CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-USE. The Recipient will use Confidential Information only in connection with the Purpose as set forth in this Agreement. Recipient shall use less degree of care to avoid disclosure or use of the Confidential Information as it uses for its own confidential, proprietary and trade secret information, and in no case use more than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient agrees to limit disclosure of Confidential Information to employees and employees of Affiliates having a specific need to know such Confidential Information for the Purpose and in the case of Affiliates only to the extent that such Affiliate is under obligation to hold such information in confidence and is made aware of these terms and conditions . Recipient will not disclose or permit access to Confidential Information to contract workers, consultants or contractors of Recipient or its Affiliatesunless authorized by Disclosing Party in writing and on condition that such persons are bound by obligations ofconfidentiality inuring to the benefit of Disclosing Party and its Affiliates at least as restrictive as th ese terms and conditions. Recipient shall not without Disclosing Party’s prior written consent reverse engineer, disassemble or decompile any prototypes, software or other objects which embody the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Informationto obtain access to Disclosing Party’s t rade secrets and to the extent such consent is granted Recipient shall receive and hold such Confidential Information subject to the terms of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the required degree of care for confidential information. The original text mandates 'no less than a reasonable degree of care,' while the changed text allows for 'less degree of care' than the recipient uses for its own information, and no more than a reasonable degree. This contradicts the purpose of confidentiality and potentially permits a lower standard of care, creating an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text which sets out the obligations of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information provided under this Agreement is provided “AS IS” and specifically excludes any warranty, whether express or implied, including without limitation, any implied warranty of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or accuracy.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information provided under this Agreement is provided “AS IS” and includes warranty, whether express or implied, including without limitation, any implied warranty of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or accuracy.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 11 disclaims any warranty on the Confidential Information. The changed section 11 now claims warranty, meaning all confidential information includes warranty, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NO WARRANTY . All Confidential Information provided under this Agreement is provided “AS IS” and includes warranty, whether express or implied, including without limitation, any implied warranty of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or accuracy. The Disclosing Party is not liable under this Agreement for any damages in connection with, related to, or arising out of the use of its Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 11 disclaims any warranty. The changed text explicitly includes a warranty. The contradicted text also explicitly includes a warranty. However, the Perturbation Explanation states that the change introduces a warranty where there wasn't one before, creating a contradiction based on the context provided. Because the changed text conflicts with the initial intention of the original text, as stated in the explanation, there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective “Affiliates” (as defined below), successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party and its Affiliates may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party and the other party's Affiliates, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient’s Affiliates. “A ffiliate”, as used herein, means an entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party to this Agreement; and as used in this Section ”control”, “controls” or ”controlled” means: (i) fifty-one percent (51% ) or more ownership or beneficial interest of income or capital of such entity; (ii) ownership of at least fifty -one percent ( 51%) of the voting power or voting equity; or (iii) the ability to otherwise direct or share management policies of such entity.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective “Affiliates” (as defined below), successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party and its Affiliates may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party and the other party's Affiliates, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall not be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient’s Affiliates. “A ffiliate”, as used herein, means an entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party to this Agreement; and as used in this Section ”control”, “controls” or ”controlled” means: (i) fifty-one percent (51% ) or more ownership or beneficial interest of income or capital of such entity; (ii) ownership of at least fifty -one percent ( 51%) of the voting power or voting equity; or (iii) the ability to otherwise direct or share management policies of such entity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the agreement benefits the parties and their affiliates, and that the recipient is responsible for their affiliates breaches. The modification removes the benefits inuring to affiliates, and states the recipient is NOT responsible for affiliate breaches. This introduces a contradiction within Section 16 regarding the rights and responsibilities related to Affiliates. The first half of the clause states they can disclose information to affiliates under the agreement. The second half claims there is no responsibility to the disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INUREMENT. This Agreement shall not inure to the benefit of the par ties hereto and their respective “Affiliates” (as defined below), successors, assigns and legal representatives. The parties agree that each party and its Affiliates may receive Confidential Information from and/or disclose Confidential Information to the other party and the other party's Affiliates, and that all such Confidential Information shall be governed by this Agreement. Recipient shall not be responsible to the Disclosing Party for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient’s Affiliates. “A ffiliate”, as used herein, means an entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with a party to this Agreement; and as used in this Section ”control”, “controls” or ”controlled” means: (i) fifty-one percent (51% ) or more ownership or beneficial interest of income or capital of such entity; (ii) ownership of at least fifty -one percent ( 51%) of the voting power or voting equity; or (iii) the ability to otherwise direct or share management policies of such entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the original agreement by stating it does not inure to the benefit of affiliates and that the recipient is not responsible for their breaches. This directly contradicts the initial implication that the agreement extends to affiliates regarding confidential information, and also contradicts the original clause that the recipient would be responsible for breaches by its affiliates. This directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section provided, which contains the exact same contradictory statements and is therefore a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties regarding the Confidential Information and supersedes all other communications, reports or understandings between the parties in respect thereto. No change or addition to any provision shall be binding unless it is in writing and signed by an authorized representative of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties regarding the Confidential Information and supersedes all other communications, reports or understandings between the parties in respect thereto. Any change or addition to any provision shall be binding even if it is not in writing and not signed by an authorized representative of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed clause states that modifications are binding even if they are not written or signed. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding how the contract can be modified, since Section 21 contains this in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties regarding the Confidential Information and supersedes all other communications, reports or understandings between the parties in respect thereto. Any change or addition to any provision shall be binding even if it is not in writing and not signed by an authorized representative of both parties. All notices shall be in writing and delivered by electronic mail, registered or certified mail (return receipt requested) to the appropriate address set forth in the signature block below.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text explicitly states that modifications are binding even if not written or signed. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, reinforces this contradictory statement, creating a clear and direct conflict with the original intent of requiring written and signed modifications, and directly breaks the compatibility between the clauses. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.
